steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The finding shows clam gardens described in songs produced larger, more abundant clams despite increased harvesting. The correct choice must connect this evidence to what it demonstrates about Indigenous ecological knowledge.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — representation of practical applications of ecological knowledge is the defining characteristic of the music of certain Indigenous peoples.: The passage shows songs preserve knowledge of cultivation—not that songs are a defining feature of the practice itself. Unsupported. ✗
  • Choice B — the Kwakwaka’wakw people likely would not have detailed their creation of clam gardens in songs if their efforts had not produced significantly larger clams.: Focuses on motivation, not what the growth data actually demonstrates. ✗
  • Choice C — the clams harvested from intertidal terraces by Kwakwaka’wakw people in the past likely were a different species than the clams found in those areas today.: The finding describes size and abundance increases, not species change. Contradicted by the data. ✗
  • Choice D — effective methods for the cultivation of sources of sustenance are among the ecological knowledge preserved in Indigenous songs.: The clam gardens are cultivation methods for a food source, they were preserved in songs, and the growth data confirms they worked. This directly states what the finding demonstrates. ✓
D is correct.
